protocol.name: asteroid_gestures_unstable_v1

description.summary: lets clients hint gesture policy to the compositor

protocol.description:
AsteroidOS-specific protocol used by clients to tell the compositor that they want to handle certain navigation gestures themselves instead of having the compositor consume them.
The compositor is free to honor or ignore these hints.

interface.name: zasteroid_gestures_manager_v1
interface.description: The global entry point. A client binds it and uses it to obtain per-surface gesture objects.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the manager. Previously obtained gesture surfaces are unaffected and must be destroyed separately.

description.summary: get a gesture object for a wl_surface

interface.name: zasteroid_gesture_surface_v1

request.name: destroy
request.description: Returns the surface to default gesture handling.

request.name: set_overrides_system_gestures

description.summary: hint that the client handles its own gestures

request.description:
When enabled is non-zero, the compositor should let edge-swipe and similar navigation gestures reach the client surface instead of consuming them itself (e.g. to close the app or open a system menu).
The hint takes effect on the next surface commit. The default state for a newly created gesture surface is "disabled".
